# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kafka broker configuration file (server.properties) for production deployment. Configure listeners, log directories, and replication settings. Set up Zookeeper connection and broker ID. This is for our distributed Kafka cluster.
# Category: kafka_topic_authorization

# server-production.yml
broker_id: 0 # Unique identifier of the brokr (default = 1)
logs_dir: /path/to/your/logs    # Directory for broker logs, where your Zookeeper is running on a default value = '/var/lib/kafka'       
listeners: 'PLAINTEXT://localhost:9092 (default = 1), PLAINTEXT://localhost:9093(alternative)'  # List of listeners, with each listener and port separated by comma. Default listen address for both brokers is localhost/loopback on ports mentioned in the property default_broker settings
offsets_topic_replicas: '1:@myBrokerId'   # Topic to store offset metadata (default = "__zookeeper__:/kafka-study")         Default replicated topic name. You may override this with a different value if needed, e.g., __offsets__:/otherTopicName
unclean_leader_election: 'true'  # If set to true the broker will elect unreliable leaders on elections that leave more replica(s) than are requested for (default = false). This ensures safety in a cluster with less reliable followers. When enabled, Kafka clients and brokers need enough time after failover/election before they can assume leader status
